title: Ensure that the --kubelet-client-certificate and --kubelet- client-key arguments are set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --kubelet-client-certificate and --kubelet- client-key arguments are set as appropriate
input_row.rationale: The apiserver, by default, does not authenticate itself to the kubelet's HTTPS endpoints. The requests from the apiserver are treated anonymously. You should set up certificate- based kubelet authentication to ensure that the apiserver authenticates itself to kubelets when submitting requests. Impact: You require TLS to be configured on apiserver as well as kubelets.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --kubelet-client-certificate and --kubelet-client-key arguments exist and they are set as appropriate. Alternative Audit kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[]}{.spec.containers[].command} {"\n"}{end}' | grep '--kubelet-client-certificate' | grep -i false If the exit code is '1', then the control isn't present / failed
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set up the TLS connection between the apiserver and kubelets. Then, edit API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the kubelet client certificate and key parameters as below. --kubelet-client-certificate=<path/to/client-certificate-file> --kubelet-client-key=<path/to/client-key-file> Default Value: By default, certificate-based kubelet authentication is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-authentication-authorization/ 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/master-node- communication/#apiserver---kubelet
input_row.impact: You require TLS to be configured on apiserver as well as kubelets.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_kubelet_client_certificate_and_key_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification in the kube-system namespace (typically managed as a static Pod on control plane nodes).
- Use the following kubectl command to retrieve the command/args for the kube-apiserver container:
  ```
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{range .items[]}{.spec.containers[].command}{"\n"}{end}'
  ```
- Alternatively, if the kube-apiserver is not managed as a Pod (rare in modern clusters), access the static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` via automation.
- Parse the command/args to ensure BOTH of the following flags are present and set to non-empty values:
  - `--kubelet-client-certificate=<path/to/client-certificate-file>`
  - `--kubelet-client-key=<path/to/client-key-file>`
- Pass Criteria:
  - Both flags exist in the kube-apiserver command/args.
  - The values for both flags are non-empty and point to valid file paths (further validation may check file existence if host access is available, but presence in config is sufficient for this check).
- Fail Criteria:
  - Either flag is missing, set to an empty value, or explicitly set to `false`.
- This check can be fully automated using Kubernetes API and kubectl, requiring no manual/host-level inspection.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
